programs.name: AI Literacy 101
programs.description: A 6-week introductory program that demystifies artificial intelligence for everyday people. Participants learn how AI works, how to use AI tools responsibly, and how to spot misinformation and bias. Delivered in community centers, libraries, and churches — in person and online.
programs.audience: Adults, seniors, and community members with no prior technology background
programs.impact: Over 500 participants completed the program. 78% reported feeling more confident using technology after completing the course.

programs.name: Youth Coder Pipeline
programs.description: An intensive 12-month coding and AI track for young people ages 12–22. Begins with Python fundamentals and progresses to building real AI-powered projects using the Heady™ platform. Includes weekly mentorship sessions, scholarship opportunities, and internship pathways with HeadySystems Inc.
programs.audience: Youth ages 12–22 in underserved communities, schools, and youth centers
programs.impact: 87 youth enrolled in the current cohort. 14 participants have received scholarships or internship placements.

programs.name: Community Hub Access
programs.description: Provides verified community organizations — schools, nonprofits, libraries, and faith communities — with free access to HeadyBuddy and core Heady™ tools. Organizations receive a community account, basic training, and ongoing support to embed AI into their own services.
programs.audience: Community organizations, nonprofits, schools, and libraries serving underserved populations
programs.impact: 42 community organizations onboarded. Collectively serving over 3,000 community members monthly.

programs.name: Senior Tech Companion
programs.description: A gentle, judgment-free program that introduces older adults (55+) to smartphones, internet safety, and basic AI tools. HeadyBuddy is customized in its Elder Companion mode — slower-paced, voice-friendly, and deeply patient. Program delivered at senior centers and in partnership with assisted living facilities.
programs.audience: Adults aged 55 and older, particularly those with limited technology experience
programs.impact: Over 200 seniors enrolled. 91% satisfaction rate. Reported improvements in social connection, independence, and confidence.
